paper_wording: The current adaptive schedule is a middle rate-distortion point between uniform gap8 and uniform gap4.
Adaptive improves PSNR, SSIM, MS-SSIM, and LPIPS over uniform gap8 on full-sequence evaluation.
Do not claim the frozen adaptive schedule is lower-rate than uniform gap8.
Stage188 additive rates compare lower-budget candidates internally and must not be numerically mixed with Stage185 schedule-packed rates.
The decoder receives original StreamSplat endpoint/base inputs, normalized time, encoded q6 keep1.0 entropy residual payload, counted half selector, and transmitted schedule/keyframe metadata.
The decoder does not receive target dense anchors, target RGB, unencoded target residuals, or oracle labels.
RGB/motion selector features are encoder-side only; the transmitted schedule is counted and sufficient for decoding.
Stage189 shows broad residual-risk hotspots and rare high-cost promotions; further selector refinement is needed for an optimized RD frontier.
If final claims require same-scope candidate RD, selected Stage188 candidates should be measured with schedule-packed keyframe streams.
